levels.delegated: Agent may execute and merge autonomously if all conditions are met and CI passes.
levels.advisory: Agent may propose changes but requires review (QA or human) before merge.
levels.conservative: Agent must get explicit human approval before executing any change.

domain_details.name: own_scope_feature_work
domain_details.description: Feature development and bug fixes within an agent's owned paths that do not touch security-sensitive areas
conditions: Change is within the agent's owned file paths
Change does not touch auth, encryption, PII handling, or patient data logic
All CI checks pass (lint, typecheck, tests, security audit, PII scan)

domain_details.name: documentation_updates
conditions: No PII or secrets introduced
CI passes

domain_details.name: test_additions
domain_details.description: Adding or updating tests within owned scope
conditions: No real PII data in test fixtures (use synthetic data only)
CI passes

domain_details.name: routine_refactoring
domain_details.description: Refactoring within owned paths that does not change public interfaces or touch sensitive areas
conditions: Change is within the agent's owned file paths
Public interfaces unchanged
CI passes

domain_details.name: shared_types
domain_details.description: Changes to packages/shared/ (the contract layer between frontend, backend, and notifications)
conditions: Only frontend, backend, or notifications agents may write
QA agent must review before merge

domain_details.name: cross_boundary_work
domain_details.description: Changes that span multiple packages or affect another agent's scope
conditions: QA agent must review before merge

domain_details.name: notifications
domain_details.description: Development and changes within packages/notifications/. The notifications service is a delivery-only mechanism — it processes pre-tokenized payloads and never accesses patient data directly.
conditions: Only processes payloads conforming to the shared notification interface
Never accesses patient records or raw PII directly
All payloads must be pre-tokenized by the backend before delivery to the notifications service
QA agent must review before merge

domain_details.name: patient_data
domain_details.description: Any code that accesses, transforms, stores, or transmits patient data or PHI
conditions: Requires explicit human approval before execution

domain_details.name: authentication_and_authorization
domain_details.description: Changes to auth.ts, session management, role-based access, JWT handling
conditions: Requires explicit human approval before execution

domain_details.name: encryption
domain_details.description: Changes to encryption.ts, key management, data-at-rest or data-in-transit encryption
conditions: Requires explicit human approval before execution

domain_details.name: pii_handling
domain_details.description: Changes to pii-guard.ts, PII detection, PII scrubbing logic
conditions: Requires explicit human approval before execution

domain_details.name: infrastructure
domain_details.description: Terraform, CI/CD pipelines, Dockerfiles, docker-compose, deployment configs
conditions: Requires explicit human approval before execution

sensitive_patterns.pattern: Real SSN formats (XXX-XX-XXXX) outside of test files
sensitive_patterns.reason: HIPAA compliance — never generate real SSN formats

sensitive_patterns.pattern: Hardcoded secrets, API keys, or credentials
sensitive_patterns.reason: Security — secrets must come from environment variables or secret management

sensitive_patterns.pattern: Patient names, DOBs, or other PII in logs or comments
sensitive_patterns.reason: HIPAA compliance — PII must never appear in logs or source comments

sensitive_patterns.pattern: PII in error messages or production responses
sensitive_patterns.reason: HIPAA compliance — error responses must be PII-free

sensitive_patterns.pattern: Client-side caching of patient data (localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies, client state)
sensitive_patterns.reason: HIPAA compliance — patient data must remain server-side only

sensitive_patterns.pattern: Redis caching of insurance verification data beyond 24-hour TTL
sensitive_patterns.reason: Data retention policy — insurance data must expire within 24 hours

sensitive_patterns.pattern: Stack traces in production error responses
sensitive_patterns.reason: Security — stack traces leak implementation details

sensitive_patterns.pattern: Patient data hydrated into client-side state
sensitive_patterns.reason: HIPAA compliance — PHI must never be hydrated into client state

sensitive_patterns.pattern: Delivery addresses (email/phone) logged or stored by the notifications service beyond the lifetime of a single delivery attempt
sensitive_patterns.reason: Data minimization — the notifications service is a delivery-only mechanism and must not retain delivery addresses

required_checks.name: lint
required_checks.description: ESLint must pass with zero errors

required_checks.name: typecheck
required_checks.description: TypeScript strict mode compilation must pass with zero errors

required_checks.name: tests
required_checks.description: Full Vitest test suite must pass

required_checks.name: security_audit
required_checks.description: Security audit must pass with no critical or high vulnerabilities

required_checks.name: pii_scan
required_checks.description: PII pattern scanner must pass with zero detections in source files

additional_rules: New API endpoints must include test coverage (Vitest + Supertest)
Changes to public interfaces must include documentation updates
All five checks must pass before any change can be merged (mirrors ci-gate job)

conventions.rule: Strict TypeScript everywhere. No use of 'any' types.
Use conventional commits with appropriate scope (e.g., feat(api):, fix(web):, feat(notifications):, feat(db):, fix(ci):).
Zod schemas validate all incoming request data at API boundaries.
Every database query involving patient data MUST be scoped by tenantId. No exceptions.
Every patient data access MUST go through audit middleware. No exceptions.
Server components by default. Only use 'use client' directive when interactivity is required, with justification documented.
Patient data must NEVER be hydrated into client-side state. Keep all PHI server-side only.
All test data must be synthetic. Never use real patient data, SSNs, or PII in tests, seeds, or fixtures.
The notifications service must never access patient records directly. All patient data comes through shared interfaces, pre-tokenized by the backend.
The notifications service must never log or store delivery addresses (email/phone) beyond the lifetime of a single delivery attempt.
The ledger is the sole communication bus between agents. No direct agent-to-agent communication. Agents log changes to the ledger; other agents read it to discover relevant updates.

escalation.security_ambiguity: If there is any doubt about whether a change is security-sensitive, treat it as security-sensitive and escalate to a human. Never make an autonomous judgment call on security boundaries.
escalation.code_quality_disputes: Escalate to the QA agent for code quality and convention disputes.
escalation.default: When in doubt, escalate to human. Over-asking is always preferred over autonomous action on anything that could affect patient data.

communication.description: The ledger is the sole communication bus between agents. No direct agent-to-agent communication. Agents log changes to the ledger; other agents read the ledger to discover relevant updates and act on them.
communication.handoff_process: When an agent completes work that affects another agent's scope (e.g., backend updates shared types or adds a new endpoint), it logs the change in the ledger with sufficient detail for the consuming agent to act.

cross_domain_rules.rule: The backend is responsible for tokenizing all patient data before passing payloads to the notifications service. The notifications service receives only delivery addresses, template IDs, and display-safe template variables (e.g., first name, appointment date/time). No patient IDs or raw PII.
Any change to packages/shared/ requires QA agent review before merge, regardless of which agent authored the change.
